$comment: Mechanically-enforced gate baselines (see EVALS.md rules). run-routing.mjs prints a gate verdict on every run and exits 1 on breach when invoked with --gate (CI does). Re-baselining is an explicit act: update these numbers in the same commit as the change that moves them, with the decision recorded in Solo (EVALS.md rule 1). Counts are absolute hits, not percentages.
note: re-baselined 2026-07-04 on ADR-0003 build-time exposure filtering (Solo todo 836): the manifest now contains ONLY exposed entries (274; twins/retired skills/denied ops never emitted) and the grader dropped the v2 twin-identity layer — a hit's service label is exactly its own. Legacy strict 222/288/318 → 203/265/303. Of 37 changed cases, 35 are pure grading-severity changes on lumenloop-expected cases whose TOP HITS ARE IDENTICAL (skills.lumenloop.* playbook hits no longer credited to the lumenloop service — that tolerance is the accept-either lane's job, 74.0% top1); the only 2 ranking changes are both improvements (top1 miss→hit, from keyword document-frequency denominators shifting when excluded ops left the per-service op sets). Skills lane unchanged 18/23. Prior baseline: routing-2026-07-03T15-14-31-853Z.json (M3/M4 operation keywords, todo 824).
